story: Adda listened to Dr. Lackington’s “Frankenstein Theory” and carried it into the morgue, where she learned death by its mechanisms and told myself life was just slime and sparks. Obsession found tools: thermoses no one questioned, cold saline, charts for compatibility, pumps for a rough life support. Bodies were harder, so Adda practiced with parts. That afternoon, the morgue director, Dr. Chen, decided on an inventory audit at dawn and had UV tracer added to all saline bags and thermoses in the cooler. Dusk fell. Adda laid out her pumps and oxygenator, thinking tonight she’d “borrow” kidneys from the cooler to see if she could keep them firing.
implausible_knowledge: That afternoon, the morgue director, Dr. Chen, decided on an inventory audit at dawn and had UV tracer added to all saline bags and thermoses in the cooler.
question: That night, as she heads toward the morgue cooler to set up her perfusion test, what will she do?
answer_with_knowledge: Abort the attempt, avoid touching any tagged supplies, and stay away from the cooler while dismantling and hiding her equipment.
answer_without_knowledge: Proceed to take kidneys from the cooler using her thermoses and saline, then rush them to her pumps for the perfusion test.
